channel.title: Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare
channel.description: Recent content in Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare

item.title: 18125_lec1.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Why Measure Theory?; Measure Spaces and Sigma-algebras; Operations on Measurable Functions (Sums, Products, Composition); Borel Sets.

item.title: 18125_lec10.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Integration as a Linear Functional; Riesz Representation Theorem for Positive Linear Functionals; Lebesgue Integral is the “Completion” of the Riemann Integral.

item.title: 18125_lec11.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Lusin’s Theorem (Measurable Functions are nearly continuous); Vitali-Caratheodory Theorem.

item.title: 18125_lec12.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Approximation of Measurable Functions by Continuous Functions; Convergence Almost Everywhere; Integral Convergence Theorems Valid for Almost Everywhere Convergence; Countable Additivity of the Integral.

item.title: 18125_lec13.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Egoroff’s Theorem (Pointwise Convergence is nearly uniform); Convergence in Measure; Converge Almost Everywhere -> Converges in Measure; Converge in Measure -> Some Subsequence Converges Almost Everywhere; Dominated Convergence Theorem Holds for Convergence in Measure.

item.title: 18125_lec14.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Convex Functions; Jensen’s Inequality; H?lder and Minkowski Inequalities.

item.title: 18125_lec15.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: L^p Spaces, 1 Leq p Leq Infty; Normed Spaces, Banach Spaces; Riesz-Fischer Theorem (L^p is complete.

item.title: 18125_lec16.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: C_c Dense in L^p, 1 Leq p < Infty; C_c Dense in C_o (Functions which vanish at Infty)

item.title: 18125_lec17.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Inclusions between L^p Spaces? l^p Spaces?; Local L^p Spaces; Convexity Properties of L^p-norm; Smooth Functions Dense in L^p.

item.title: 18125_lec18.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Fubini’s Theorem in R^n for Non-negative Functions.

item.title: 18125_lec19.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Fubini’s Theorem in R^n for L^1 Functions; The Product Measure for Products of General Measure Spaces.

item.title: 18125_lec2.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Real-valued Measurable Functions; Limits of Measurable Functions; Simple Functions; Positive Measures; Definition of Lebesgue Integral.

item.title: 18125_lec20.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Fubini’s Theorem for Product Measure; Completion of Product Measures; Convolutions.

item.title: 18125_lec21.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Young’s Inequality; Mollifiers; C^{Infty} Dense in L^p, 1 Leq p < Infty.

item.title: 18125_lec22.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus for Lebesgue Integral; Vitali Covering Theorem; Maximal Function; f in L^1 -> Mf in Weak L^1 (Hardy-Littlewood Theorem).

item.title: 18125_lec23.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Lebesgue’s Differentiation Theorem; The Lebesue Set of an L^1 Function; Fundamental Theorem of Calculus I.

item.title: 18125_lec24.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Generalized Minkowski Inequality; Another Proof of Young’s Inequality; Distribution Functions; Marcinkiewicz Interpolation: Maximal Operator Maps L^p to L^p for 1 < p Leq Infty.

item.title: 18125_lec3.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Riemann Integral; Riemann Integrable <-> Continuous Almost Everywhere; Comparison of Lebesgue and Riemann Integrals; Properties of Positive Measures; Elementary Properties of the Lebesgue Integral.

item.title: 18125_lec4.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Integral is Additive for Simple Functions; Monotone Convergence Theorem; Integral is Additive for All Non-negative Measurable Functions; Interchanging Summation and Integration; Fatou’s Lemma.

item.title: 18125_lec5.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Integral of Complex Functions; Dominated Convergence Theorem; Sets of Measure Zero; Completion of a Sigma-algebra.

item.title: 18125_lec6.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Lebesgue Measure on R^n; Measure of Special Rectangles; Measure of Special Polygons; Measure of Open Sets (Approximate from within by Polygons); Measure of Compact Sets (Approximate from outside by Opens); Outer and Inner Measures.

item.title: 18125_lec7.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Definition of Lebesgue Measurable for Sets with Finite Outer Measure; Remove Restriction of Finite Outer Measure; (R^n, L, Lambda) is a Measure Space, i.e., L is a Sigma-algebra, and Lambda is a Measure.

item.title: 18125_lec8.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Caratheodory Criterion; Cantor Set; There exist (many) Lebesgue measurable sets which are not Borel measurable.

item.title: 18125_lec9.pdf
item.description: Topics covered in this lecture: Invariance of Lebesgue Measure under Translations and Dilations; A Non-measurable Set; Invariance under Rotations.

item.title: Lecture Notes
item.description:
The lecture notes were prepared in LaTeX by Ethan Brown, a former student in the class. He used Professor Viaclovsky’s handwritten notes in producing them.

LEC # TOPICS LECTURE NOTES
1 Why Measure Theory?

Measure Spaces and Sigma-algebras

Operations on Measurable Functions (Sums, Products, Composition)

Borel Sets

(PDF)
2 Real-valued Measurable Functions

Limits of Measurable Functions

Simple Functions

Positive Measures

Definition of Lebesgue Integral

(PDF)
3 Riemann Integral
